item.title: Moderator post - info
item.content:encoded:
Localised your image, please remember to upload them on the page you make and not just hotlink from sandbox in future

Licensing looks good though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Barring the logistic problems we discussed while workshopping the article, the cartoonish amounts of gore still just doesn't engage me.

You say often that you're not "using gore for the sake of gore" and I believe you have very thought out reasons and researched references to justify that, but the premise of my statement doesn't interact on that level.

The article seems to be focused on the ritual and how complex it is, presupposing a lot of interest I don't feel the hook buys you. Ultimately, your choice to highlight the dark origins of marriage isn't appealing to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hahaha.

"Truth is stranger than fiction", right?

I'm glad you took the time to check it out. I learned a bit more than I expected to while building this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeesh. Did a little studying, and the various quirky traditions of contemporary weddings have some bloody, rapey, paranoid origins. Point well taken. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can see how it would be perceived that way. However I do want to point out that nearly every event listed in the ritual is based on traditional wedding ceremonies. A lot of the things done are outdated and their origins are not well known.

There are quite a few Easter eggs I didn't fully explain sprinkled throughout the ceremony itself, from the color change of the flowers to the execution of the bridesmaids. I did a LOT of research for this SCP, and while I didn't elaborate on every bit, I can tell you that nearly every part of it traces back to a tradition relating to marriage.

I don't want to spoil everything, but if you're curious I can give you some hints on what to look for:

  1. The significance of a purely red and white bouquet.
  2. The reason the bouquet is thrown.
  3. The significance of bridesmaids and their purpose.
  4. Why the groom stands on the right during the ceremony.

There are a few others, but I just wanted to clarify that it's not just mindless gore for the sake of gore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I applaud you for resisting the temptation to name this SCP "The Red Wedding". This is a very good SCP and I really like seeing the parameters of this phenomenon. We know how to prevent it, we know how it works, and we know what happens if we fail to prevent it. You don't see that often in skips. I like this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will always have a soft spot for skips that only become anomalous when you do something very specific and not entirely usual with them. You could give these to a friend, display them on a table, make bouquets of them for a funeral or just let them grow into oranges (if they do without the event concluding, anyway), and absolutely nothing bad would happen. But try and make a wedding look nice with them and look out, Chucky Barbara. :B

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
verses Solomon 8:6 through 8:7.

Considering that not all of us can recall Biblical quotes from memory or have time to Google it, you can afford to provide a footnote for explanation.

I know you provided a translation eventually, but I think a footnote would make it more intuitive than leaving it at the last third of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally love the religious background of the Skip, love the concept, mostly love the execution. But the description of the effects- the gore factor- seemed cartoonish to me. Broke me out of the narrative. I think most of that could be replaced with 'subject will then engage in ritualistic mutilation.' Tentative no-vote for now, but I'll be keeping an eye on this.

item.title: Interesting...
item.content:encoded: I like that the Foundation actually did their homework on this thing and bothered to translate the verses… but I also wonder if too much is explained? It also feels like the ritual is just needlessly complicated. I'm not sure. I like it far too much to downvote, but not quite enough to upvote at this point.

item.title: Thanks for viewing my SCP.
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate any views, votes or feedback on my SCP. Thanks for reading.

Source for the image used is https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Orange_(fruit)#/media/File%3AOrange_Blossom.JPG.
